research.interface: GitHub GraphQL API (POST /graphql, cursor pagination, point-based rate limits)

official_sources.title: Forming calls with GraphQL - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: single endpoint https://api.github.com/graphql; 'Authorization: bearer TOKEN' (lowercase bearer); all queries/mutations use POST with a JSON body whose 'query' member is a string and whose variables travel in a separate 'variables' object; GET is only for introspection

official_sources.title: Using pagination in the GraphQL API - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: first/after forward pagination; pageInfo { hasNextPage, endCursor, hasPreviousPage, startCursor }; pass endCursor as the next request's after argument and continue until hasNextPage is false; 'The maximum number of items you can fetch using the first or last argument is 100'

official_sources.title: Rate limits and node limits for the GraphQL API - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: point-based primary rate limit; rateLimit query field with limit/cost/remaining/used/resetAt; x-ratelimit-limit/-remaining/-used/-reset (UTC epoch seconds)/-resource response headers with x-ratelimit-resource always 'graphql'; clients must supply first or last (1-100) on connections

research.language_rationale: The asset dashboard consuming this inventory is a TypeScript service, and GitHub's GraphQL surface is plain JSON-over-POST with no official first-party JS GraphQL SDK required — the docs themselves demonstrate raw HTTP calls — so typed fetch against /graphql with node:test acceptance is the idiomatic zero-dependency integration for the documented cursor/pageInfo and rateLimit contracts.

verified_facts: GraphQL requests are POSTs to the single /graphql endpoint with a JSON body of query (string) plus a separate variables object; the docs authenticate with the lowercase 'bearer' scheme.
Connections paginate with first/after and pageInfo { hasNextPage, endCursor }: pass endCursor verbatim as after and stop when hasNextPage is false; first/last are capped at 100.

The primary rate limit is point-based and observable both through the rateLimit query field (limit, cost, remaining, used, resetAt) and x-ratelimit-* response headers (reset in UTC epoch seconds, resource 'graphql').
A 200 GraphQL response may carry both partial data and an errors array whose entries expose message, type, and path; HTTP-level failures instead use GitHub's {message} JSON error body.
GitHub requires a User-Agent header on API requests.
